mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2%) triggers central bank tightening expectations, increasing equity discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST > 2000k units) combined with declining consumer spending (RSAFS < 350bn) signals weak demand fundamentals, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS < 100k) and rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) indicates labor market deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal incorrectly predicts positive returns in > 35% of triggered cases, or the joint effect's IC falls below -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO < 105) signals manufacturing weakness, reducing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of instances, or the IC drops below -0.03 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1 > 3% annualized) indicates robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal incorrectly predicts negative returns in > 40% of cases, or the IC falls below 0.02 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 4.5%) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS > 150k) signals a tightening labor market, pushing inflation and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns in > 30% of cases, or the joint effect's IC drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the IC drops below -0.03.
